topic: Recruiter search tests with real resume teardowns
thesis: Turn a timely job-search pain point into one useful long-form teardown and several short proof clips.

sourceNotes.title: YouTube Partner Program eligibility
sourceNotes.note: Use YouTube as a monetization target, but optimize first for site conversions.

sourceNotes.title: Pew Research Center Social Media Fact Sheet
sourceNotes.note: YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok are large discovery channels for job-search education.

youtube.title: Recruiter search tests with real resume teardowns | Resume Teardown
youtube.script:
Welcome to the Resume Roast! 
[Intro music, visuals of pain points] 
In today’s episode, we’re diving into the job-search jungle with our trusty ‘Signal scored’ resumes! 
[Show an applicant spreadsheet] 
Goes like this: 
1. The classic 'I am a team player' line. 
*Cut to recruiter baffled, roasting: 'Is your team a football team or something? Tell me the position!'*

2. The vague 'Responsible for customer satisfaction.' Let's repair that – slap a 'boosted retention by 30% using personalized follow-ups!' 
*Recognizing a winner vibe; cue the hero music.*

3. Cluster of generic bullet points; tell me you’re looking for a job without telling me you're searching... *reel in on these boring metrics—yawn.* But guys, you do NOT have to settle for mediocrity! 
[Quick cut to Signal mascot shaking its head in disapproval]

Now—here’s the secret ingredient! Use our free Signal Score to find out what you’re doing right and wrong! [CTA animation] Grab it now! [End with humor} 
Thank you for tuning: Subscribe for the next episode! 
[Outro music].

storyboard.visual: Animated Signal mascot enters a chaotic job application scene.
storyboard.dialogue: Welcome to the Resume Roast, where we turn your resume pain into gain!

storyboard.visual: Show a 'typical resume' on screen while the recruiter reacts.
storyboard.dialogue: 'Responsible for customer satisfaction!' Hmm, what does that even mean?

storyboard.visual: Cut to a failed resume with the playing field of job applications.
storyboard.dialogue: Don't spell mediocrity; we can innovate instead!

storyboard.visual: Signal mascot presents the Signal Score chart.
storyboard.dialogue: Want to rescue your resume? Get your FREE Signal Score!

youtube.seoTitle: Recruiter search tests with real resume teardowns | Resume Teardown with Signal by ATSHacker
youtube.description: A funny recruiter-style teardown of why a qualified resume can be hard to find, how to translate a job description into resume proof, and where Signal fits without fake claims.
youtube.cta: Paste the job description and check your free Signal score before you apply.

scriptSections.label: Cold open
scriptSections.script: Recruiter search tests with real resume teardowns This resume looks professional, which is why the mistake is dangerous. On screen, it feels polished. In a recruiter search, the proof is still hard to find.
scriptSections.visual: Cold open with comic red stamp, quick zoom, Signal mascot reaction, and resume/JD split screen.

scriptSections.label: Open loop
scriptSections.script: We are not adding fake experience. We are checking whether the resume explains the real experience in the same language as the target job.
scriptSections.visual: Show the weak resume score, then hold the improved score as the unresolved payoff.

scriptSections.label: Translate the job description
scriptSections.script: Before touching the resume, pull out the role title, tools, responsibilities, metrics, and proof signals the job actually asks for.
scriptSections.visual: Resume on left, job description on right, mismatched terms highlighted with funny labels.

scriptSections.label: Recruiter search test
scriptSections.script: If a recruiter searches for the first three important terms from the job description, this resume should produce useful hits. Right now, the source bullet is too broad.
scriptSections.visual: Search console typing the first three job-description terms and showing weak matches.

scriptSections.label: Resume crime scene
scriptSections.script: Now compare the resume line by line. The weak bullet is not dishonest. It is just too vague to carry the role-specific proof.

scriptSections.label: Why it gets missed
scriptSections.script: A polished sentence can still hide the most important evidence. Recruiters skim for tools, scope, and outcomes because those make fit easy to verify.
scriptSections.visual: Zoom into the weak bullet and place missing proof labels beside it.

scriptSections.label: Live bullet fix
scriptSections.script: The fix is to say the real work clearly: what tool, what scope, what audience, and what result. Same person, clearer evidence.

scriptSections.label: Score reveal
scriptSections.script: Once the evidence matches the role language, the example score moves up. The point is not gaming a robot. The point is making real fit easier to see.
scriptSections.visual: Score meter jumps from low match to high match with before/after resume panels.

scriptSections.label: Trust and CTA
scriptSections.script: Signal automates this comparison. Paste the job description, upload the resume, and check the free score first. If the gap is real, then the paid resume and cover letter package makes sense.
scriptSections.visual: Free Signal score page, trust badges, and bundle CTA.

shorts.title: This resume sentence is quietly expensive
shorts.script: Stop here. This is the line making a qualified person look generic. The job wants Demand Generation Manager: HubSpot workflows, LinkedIn Ads, and CAC analysis. The resume says, Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails. That is real experience, but it is wearing a beige jacket. Signal would only pull out the proof already there: built lead scoring and retargeting that cut CAC 32 percent. Now a recruiter can find what they were already searching for. Check your free Signal score before you apply.
storyboard: Case: Marketing Coordinator -> Demand Gen Candidate against Demand Generation Manager.
Open on the resume with a red audit stamp over the weak bullet.
Cut to the target job title and three role terms.
Signal appears with a side-eye bubble, but the resume remains the main visual.
Circle the weak bullet and stamp why it fails.
Reveal the rewritten bullet as a receipt, not a fake addition.
End with the score jump and free Signal score CTA.

signalLines.problem: Readable is not the same as searchable.
signalLines.teardown: Roast the bullet, rescue the person.

weakBullets: Supported email and social campaigns across multiple channels.
Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails.
Coordinated paid social assets with design and sales.
props.beforeBullet: Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails.
props.afterBullet: Built HubSpot lead-scoring and LinkedIn retargeting segments that cut CAC 32% and lifted MQL-to-SQL conversion 18%.
props.problemPunchline: The job has clues. The resume is whispering.
props.teardownPunchline: This is not a skill problem. It is a labeling problem.

jobDescription.summary: TargetCo is hiring a Demand Generation Manager who can connect practical execution with measurable business outcomes.

Partner cross-functionally and communicate progress clearly to stakeholders.

shorts.title: I searched Salesforce and this resume vanished
shorts.script: Recruiter search test. I type Salesforce. Then pipeline generation. Then quota attainment. The job description is basically telling us what to look for. But this resume says, Responsible for sales opportunities in assigned territory. That may be true, but it is job-search fog with a name badge. It gives the search box nothing useful. If the real work was generated 1.8 million in qualified Salesforce pipeline, say that plainly. 37 to 88. Same work. Searchable signal. Run the free Signal score before you send it.
storyboard: Case: Account Executive Resume against Mid-Market Account Executive.
Open with a recruiter search box typing the first keyword.
Show zero useful matches on the resume.
Type two more role terms and show the same problem.
Signal points from the search box to the weak bullet.
Rewrite the bullet and rerun the search.
Close with the score jump and free score CTA.

props.hook1: If I search HubSpot, does your resume even show up?
props.subline: Signal compares resume language to the target job.
props.voiceover_text: Recruiter search test. I type Salesforce. Then pipeline generation. Then quota attainment. The job description is basically telling us what to look for. But this resume says, Responsible for sales opportunities in assigned territory. That may be true, but it is job-search fog with a name badge. It gives the search box nothing useful. If the real work was generated 1.8 million in qualified Salesforce pipeline, say that plainly. 37 to 88. Same work. Searchable signal. Run the free Signal score before you send it.
props.subhook: That is how qualified people disappear.

signalLines.problem: The proof exists. It is not findable.
signalLines.teardown: Give the search a real term.

weakBullets: Managed customer conversations and followed up with prospects.
Responsible for sales opportunities in assigned territory.
Worked with marketing and customer success on accounts.
props.beforeBullet: Responsible for sales opportunities in assigned territory.
props.afterBullet: Generated $1.8M in qualified Salesforce pipeline through 42 monthly discovery calls and MEDDICC-based account prioritization.
props.problemPunchline: A recruiter cannot search for the thought you meant.
props.teardownPunchline: This is where vague language quietly loses.
props.fixPunchline: The keyword now has proof behind it.

shorts.title: The job post gave the answer key
shorts.hook: The job post gave the answer key.
shorts.script: The job description is an open-book test. It asks for React, TypeScript, and Next.js. This resume answers with, Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools. That is a shrug in bullet form. The honest fix is specific: shipped React and TypeScript checkout work that reduced drop-off 14 percent. No fake experience. No stuffing. Just the same evidence in the language of the role. Before: 41. After: 90. Check your free Signal score before applying.
storyboard: Case: Frontend Engineer Resume against Frontend Software Engineer.
Open on the job description with answer-key highlights.
Slide the resume beside it and show the weak mismatch.
Highlight each missing role term in a different color.
Signal translates resume proof into job-description language.
Reveal the rewritten bullet with before/after color contrast.
End with the score jump and free Signal score CTA.
